rulings.Case Title: MILAN BACOKA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Warren Pumps, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (summary judgment), 2 (negligence), 4 (strict liability) and 7 (loss of consortium) and off calendar as to issues 3, 5, 6 and 8 (false representation, premises owner/contractor liability, civil battery and punitive damages). As to issues 1, 2, 4 and 7, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bacoka was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Bacoka¿s August 2011 deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories (specifically the identification and discussion of defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories) preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to issues 3, 5, 6 and 8, plaintiffs have dismissed their causes of action for false representation, premises owner/contractor liability and civil battery and their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Gregory Janiszewski¿s deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Luchetti¿s deposition testimony and Mr. Hamilton¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Alex Janiszewski was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Hamilton red dot all-purpose joint compound) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Gregory Janiszewski¿s deposition testimony and declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s moving papers failed to make an issue of substantial factor causation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SAM HASTY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Hasty was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Hasty¿s declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ISAAC SLADE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Dal-Tile Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections based on the execution of Mr. Turner¿s document are overruled, however his statements are insufficient to sustain defendant¿s initial burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar as they do not negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claim. Mr. Slade¿s deposition testimony and declaration and defendant¿s March 2011 and January 1998 responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration are overruled. Mr. Ay¿s declaration is admissible regarding dry products and that when it is mixed generates dust at various levels. Defendant¿s objections to the American Olean catalogs are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to Jeffrey Schott¿s deposition testimony are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GLENN CRESSY VS. HOPEMAN BROTHERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Brayton Purcell¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell notifies Mr. Cressy of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45am. A representative from Brayton Purcell is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)
